When I try to run "Format Document" in my vscode window for python files, it says I have no formatter for python files installed. I want it to use "yapf" (available on my PATH) to format the file, how can I do that?

Create a file test.py that runs a subprocess `elf_loader_sandbox --evergreen_content=. --evergreen_library=libnplb.so` and passes any flags given to test.py directly to the elf_loader_sandbox process. The script should output any logs and crashes elf_loader_sandbox outputs to their respective streams, as if we ran elf_loader_sandbox directly.

For each of the targets listed in the test_targets section of @cobalt/build/testing/targets/linux-x64x11-modular/test_targets.json, build it with `autoninja -C out/linux-x64x11-modular_devel <test_target>`, then if it builds run it with `DISPLAY=:20 out/linux-x64x11-modular_devel/<test_target> > output.txt`, unless a file cobalt/testing/filters/linux-x64x11-modular/<test_target>_loader_filter.json exists, in which case you should skip it. To analyze the tests, read the last few lines of output.txt. If the tests crash, filter those tests and run it again. Make sure you also filter tests from previous crashes in the same suite if it happens multiple times.

For each of the targets listed in the test_targets section of @cobalt/build/testing/targets/linux-x64x11-modular/test_targets.json, build it with `autoninja -C 
  out/linux-x64x11-modular_devel <test_target>`, then if it builds run it with `DISPLAY=:20 out/linux-x64x11-modular_devel/<test_target> > output.txt`, unless a file 
  cobalt/testing/filters/linux-x64x11-modular/<test_target>_loader_filter.json exists, in which case you should skip it. To analyze the tests, read the last few lines of 
  output.txt. If the tests crash, filter those tests and run it again. Make sure you also filter tests from previous crashes in the same suite if it happens multiple times. Once the test suite finishes a run and lists its failing tests, create a file cobalt/testing/filters/linux-x64x11-modular/<test_target>_loader_filter.json with a list of all the failing tests in the same format as i.e. cobalt/testing/filters/linux-x64x11-modular/base_unittests_loader_filter.json.
/quit
/quit

For each of the targets listed in @cobalt/build/testing/targets/linux-x64x11-modular/test_targets.json under "test_targets", run `DISPLAY=:20 out/linux-x64x11-modular_devel/<test_target> --gtest_filter=-*` where <test_target> is the string after the colon (:), i.e. for "base:base_perftests_loader" the test_target is base_perftests_loader. Whenever the test suite fails with "undefined symbol: SbEventHandle", change the main entry point of the test to closely resemble how it's defined in @@starboard/testing/run_all_unittests_starboard.cc. i.e. for url_unittests, @url/BUILD.gn contains the definition for the target with @url/run_all_unittests.cc containing the main definition. In this case, you should create a new file  url/run_all_unittests_starboard.cc which uses STARBOARD_WRAP_SIMPLE_MAIN but still does the necessary setup (in this case calling mojo::core::Init()). Once you've created that, change the GN target to use that source file instead, and rebuild and rerun the target in the same way to see if you've fixed the issue.

For each of the targets listed in @cobalt/build/testing/targets/linux-x64x11-modular/test_targets.json under "test_targets", run `DISPLAY=:20 out/linux-x64x11-modular_devel/<test_target> --gtest_filter=-*` where <test_target> is the string after the colon (:), i.e. for "base:base_perftests_loader" the test_target is base_perftests_loader. Whenever the test suite fails with "undefined symbol: SbEventHandle", change the main entry point of the test to closely resemble how it's defined in @@starboard/testing/run_all_unittests_starboard.cc. i.e. for url_unittests, @url/BUILD.gn contains the definition for the target with @url/run_all_unittests.cc containing the main definition. In this case, you should create a new file  url/run_all_unittests_starboard.cc which uses STARBOARD_WRAP_SIMPLE_MAIN but still does the necessary setup (in this case calling mojo::core::Init()). Once you've created that, change the GN target to use that source file instead, and rebuild and rerun the target in the same way to see if you've fixed the issue. You should rebuild using `autoninja -C out/linux-x64x11-modular_devel <target_name>`. Note that the main definition may not be in a file named run_all_unittests.cc in the target, and you may have to look for other sources, i.e. base_perftests uses the test_support_perf dependency.
/quit
For each of the test_targets listed in @cobalt/build/testing/targets/linux-x64x11-modular/test_targets.json after viz_unittests_loader, find where the target's main entry point is defined

For each of the test_targets listed in @cobalt/build/testing/targets/linux-x64x11-modular/test_targets.json after viz_unittests_loader, find where the target's main entry point is defined. Note that for each foo_bar_loader target, GN will list the foo_bar target instead, and that will likely contain a source with the main entry point.
Ensure you find the actual source file with the entry point for each target, not just the dependency target that contains the entry point.

For each of the files you've identified, make changes to them to run with Starboard as was done with @@components/viz/test/run_all_perftests.cc. You can see the changes made to that file with `git diff components/viz/test/run_all_perftests.cc`. If a file with an entry point already uses the STARBOARD_WRAP_SIMPLE_MAIN macro, you don't need to edit it.

You can see the changes made to that file with `git diff components/viz/test/run_all_perftests.cc`. /quit

For each of my staged .cc files, if it doesn't already end in _starboard.cc, add _starboard to the end of the filename (so foo/bar/baz.cc becomes foo/bar/baz_starboard.cc) and update the GN target that file appears in to instead use the _starboard.cc suffixed file when is_cobalt_hermetic_build is true.
/quit
